target.name: Cognition
target.founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cognition is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Scott Wu, Steven Hao, and Walden Yan that has raised $175M+. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables spanning Seed and Series A rounds, including Thinking Machines Lab ($12.0B), Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), Sakana AI ($1.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M).

Two valuation methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($175M+) as a proxy for base value and applying a standard 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implies a value of $12M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable companies yields a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $12.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous stage progression and capital efficiency across the peer set.

The triangulation produces a median estimate of $1.0B with a low bound of $10M and high bound of $2.3B. The stage-progression method significantly underweights relative to peer comparables, likely because the $5M proxy understates current enterprise value given $175M+ in actual capital raised. Key uncertainties include revenue or user traction data not provided, the extent of product differentiation within a crowded frontier AI landscape, and whether Cognition's raised capital reflects pre-revenue valuation inflation common to the sector. A narrower range would require operational metrics.
